input.prompt: I want to run only the tests with the Category=Integration trait in this xUnit project using dotnet test.

data.content: I want to run only the tests with the Category=Integration trait in this xUnit project using dotnet test.
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-30T01:23:57.711Z</current_datetime>

I want to run only the tests with the Category=Integration trait in this xUnit project using dotnet test.

input.initialPrompt: I want to run only the tests with the Category=Integration trait in this xUnit project using dotnet test.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run xUnit tests filtered by Category=Integration

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run xUnit tests filtered by Category=Integration

data.reasoningText: The user wants to run only tests with Category=Integration trait in their xUnit project. Let me look at the project files to understand the setup, then run the filtered tests.

arguments.description: Run xUnit tests filtered by Category=Integration

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-b1193304bad34db7aec38bec2254bf66 && dotnet test --filter \"Category=Integration\" --no-restore 2>&1 | head -60", "description": "Run xUnit tests filtered by Category=Integration", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.description: Run xUnit tests filtered by Category=Integration

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run filtered tests with verbose output

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run filtered tests with verbose output

data.reasoningText: The output is empty, which seems odd. Let me try without `--no-restore` and with more verbose output.

arguments.description: Run filtered tests with verbose output

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-b1193304bad34db7aec38bec2254bf66 && dotnet test --filter \"Category=Integration\" -v normal 2>&1", "description": "Run filtered tests with verbose output", "initial_wait": 45}

toolArgs.description: Run filtered tests with verbose output
